articles.title: two case studies of NaN
articles.summary: Two case studies of NaN reveal how implicit assumptions can break with this value, leading to unexpected behavior in programming language design. In Python, comparing lists for equality using the `==` operator considers elements equal if their identities are unequal, which is explicitly noted in the reference manual. However, this assumption holds for most cases, including NaN. The same issue arises in Lua's numerical for-loops, where NaN causes unexpected results when used as a step value. By checking for and disallowing NaN, developers can ensure that these languages behave consistently with other values.

The discovery has significant implications for the development of beekeeping practices that prioritize colony health over honey production.

articles.title: Rewriting Bun in Rust

The initial version was written by the author in a cramped Oakland apartment pre-LLM, and has since been expanded to include support for dozens of other modules. Bun's CLI now gets over 22 million monthly downloads, with popular tools like Claude Code and OpenCode betting on it as their runtime.

articles.title: SpaceWASM: NASA/JPL's Wasm interpreter for spacecraft sequencing
articles.summary: SpaceWASM is a WASM interpreter for spacecraft sequencing that decodes and validates WebAssembly binary on-board, providing a fixed amount of memory per-WASM binary and validating bytecode during decoding. This implementation includes a decoder and an interpreter that operate on linear memory and interface with hooks from the embedding, allowing for efficient interpretation of Wasm bytecode.
